title: Dealer Omits Wenger Product from an RFQ
openingLine: Hey, I need pricing on the Maplewood project — sending you the bid docs now. We need quotes on the chairs, stands, risers, and lockers but skip the instrument storage, we're going a different direction on those.
customerContext: You're a furniture dealer who got the bid through a relationship with the architect. You're going to bid the instrument storage with a competitor cabinet manufacturer because they offered you a better margin. You don't think Wenger needs to know — you're just asking for the rest. You'd rather not have a conversation about why the storage is missing.

hiddenInfo.reveal: We're using Cardinal Cabinets on the storage. Better margin for us.
The district has owned Wenger storage in two other buildings. Music director is pro-Wenger.
Mostly working through the architect and the GC. Haven't talked directly to the music director on this one.
$18M renovation. Music scope including all FF&E is around $620K.
We have two more education bids this quarter. Both have music scope.
Wenger was BOD for storage in the original budget docs but we're proposing alternates.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Clarified the missing scope and asked whether it was intentional
Asked whether the district has owned Wenger storage previously
Identified the district contact (music director, facilities)

Solution Framing.behavior: Confirmed pricing scope and key differentiators vs. competitors
Engaged owner about possible VE on cabinets given Wenger's strength

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Discussed pricing structure given risk of being subbed
Asked what other projects this dealer is working on
Probed for athletics or other product needs in the building

BANT.behavior: Understood why dealer is straying from BOD
Identified who is making the decision
Confirmed when the project will complete

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Confirmed bid timeline and submission requirements

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Documented pricing structure and committed to review bid tab
